ocr.text: The first table lists the sources of heat along with their estimated heat load and heat sink location, including Atomic oven with
an estimated heat load of 20–30 W located at 300 K outside dilution refrigerator, DC wires with 3 mW located at multiple
stages, coax cables with 5 mW also at multiple stages, laser beams with 1.8 mW at the 4 K stage, and RF dielectric dissipation
with 5 mW at the 100 mK stage. The second table presents the composition of various lignin types with their respective
percentages of Carbon (C), Hydrogen (H), Nitrogen (N), and Degree of Unsaturation. Kraft lignin has 62.2% C, 6.1% H, 0.9%
N, and a degree of unsaturation of 3. The [Py][Ac]-K. lignin contains 63.7% C, 5.8% H, 1.4% N, and also a degree of
unsaturation of 3. [Mim][Ac]-K. lignin holds 63.5% C, 6.0% H, 3.7% N, and a degree of unsaturation of 3.
Source of heat  Estimated heat load  Heat sink location
Atomic oven  20–30 W  300 K outside dilution refrigerator
DC wires  3 mW  Multiple stages
Coax cables  5 mW  Multiple stages
Laser beams  1.8 mW  4 K stage
RF dielectric dissipation  5 mW  100 mK stage
PIL  C (%)  H (%)  N (%)  Degree of Unsaturation
Kraft lignin  62.2  6.1  0.9  3
[Mim][Ac]-K. lignin  63.5  6.0  3.7  3
[Pyrr][Ac]-K. lignin  64.5  7.2  4.0  3
[Pyrr][Ac]-CS lignin  52.1  10.3  10.1  1
The [Pyrr][Ac]-K. lignin has 64.5% C, 7.2% H, 4.0% N, and a degree of unsaturation of 3, while the [Pyrr][Ac]-CS lignin
shows 52.1% C, 10.3% H, 10.1% N, and a degree of unsaturation of 1. Finally, EF-CS contains 43.0% C, 5.7% H, 0.9% N, and
a degree of unsaturation of 2.
